scope: Wire the AgenC TUI bridge's per-tool renderToolResultMessage to structured renderers — Bash, Edit, FileRead, Write, Grep, Glob — plus a cross-cutting tool-error renderer, replacing the generic <Box><Text>{text}</Text></Box> stub that previously routed every tool result. The upstream UserToolSuccessMessage at agenc/upstream/components/messages/UserToolResultMessage/UserToolSuccessMessage.tsx:108 already calls tool.renderToolResultMessage on the bridge tool object — so the dispatch path is live; the bridge tool is the broken layer. Each routed tool gets a tagged content envelope (e.g. <bash-stdout>, <edit-diff>, <read-content>, <write-summary>, <grep-matches>, <glob-paths>, <tool-error>) produced by message-adapter.formatStructuredToolResult / formatStructuredToolError and consumed by a local React renderer in tool-stubs.tsx that mirrors the visual contract of the corresponding upstream component (UserBashOutputMessage, FileEditToolDiff/StructuredDiffList, FileReadTool/UI, FileWriteTool/UI, GrepTool/UI, GlobTool/UI, FallbackToolUseErrorMessage). The local-renderer-not-direct-import deviation is forced by the upstream chain pulling bun:bundle and require('../memdir/teamMemPaths.js') into vitest's source resolver; the wire shape stays upstream-compatible so a future flip to direct upstream dispatch is a per-renderer one-file change.

approvedExceptions.reason: TypeScript `unknown` is the type-safe top type used in dispatch type signatures (e.g. `value: unknown` for input validation, `(node as { type: unknown }).type` for opaque React element introspection in tests). It is not the shortcut sense of unknown work — it is the canonical safe type that would otherwise be replaced by `any`, which is strictly worse.

Grep/Glob test fixtures use the literal string 'TODO' as realistic search-pattern data (e.g. `pattern: 'TODO'` and a fixture line `// TODO`). This is the kind of content a developer would actually grep for. It is fixture data, not a checklist marker.

Test files use the word `stub` as the canonical vitest/jest term for a vi.mock substitute (`Box`/`Text` stub of `../agenc/upstream/ink.js`). This is industry-standard testing terminology, not a shortcut indicating unfinished implementation work. The bridge tests document why the stub exists (the upstream chain pulls bun:bundle / memdir/teamMemPaths.js which vitest cannot resolve from raw source).

scopeOuts.agentTaskTools: AgenC's agent layer uses spawn_agent (a different lifecycle than upstream's Task/AgentTool). Dispatching agent results requires a separate ABI — the bridge would need to track parent/child relationships and structured progress, not just a result envelope. Tracked for follow-up.
scopeOuts.mcpTools: MCP tools register with dynamic mcp__<server>__<tool> names. Exact-case dispatch cannot match these without a prefix matcher; adding one widens the dispatch contract beyond exact-name routing. Tracked for follow-up.
scopeOuts.todoAndPlanTools: TodoWrite, EnterPlanMode, ExitPlanMode have their own dedicated AgenC UI surfaces (todo panel and plan-mode banner) that render outside the per-tool result-message dispatch path. Their result envelopes are intentionally minimal and route through unknown-tool-generic-fallback.
scopeOuts.shellSubprocessTools: exec_command, write_stdin route through the long-running shell session UI (separate from Bash one-shot output). Result envelopes intentionally fall through to the generic stub via unknown-tool-generic-fallback; structured rendering would require a session-aware renderer that is out of scope.
scopeOuts.controlFlowTools: Sleep, Monitor, EnterWorktree, ExitWorktree, AskUserQuestion produce minimal text status. They intentionally route through unknown-tool-generic-fallback to keep the contract focused on tool families with non-trivial structured output.

requiredBehaviors: createBridgeTool('Bash') returns a tool whose renderToolResultMessage delegates to the upstream Bash output component (UserBashOutputMessage or its equivalent) when given a Bash tool result content shape

The toolResult content shape passed to the upstream component carries stdout, stderr, and exit code separately — not joined into one string — so the upstream renderer can color stderr distinctly and surface non-zero exit codes
For non-Bash tool names the bridge tool keeps the existing generic <Text> rendering (no regression for unrouted tools)
message-adapter.ts formatStructuredToolResult for exec_command_end produces a content shape that the bridge Bash tool's renderToolResultMessage can consume
edgeCases: Bash result with empty stdout AND empty stderr renders the exit-code line and a no-output indicator without crashing
Bash result with non-zero exit code is visually distinct from zero exit code in the rendered output
Bash result with stderr but empty stdout still shows the stderr block
Bash result with stdout containing ANSI escape sequences renders without re-interpreting them as terminal control
Bash result whose stdout exceeds the runtime maxResultSizeChars is truncated rather than crashing the renderer
renderToolResultMessage receives the legacy single-string content (from a tool that did not go through formatStructuredToolResult) and falls back to the generic Text renderer instead of throwing

rows.deviation: The matrix originally pointed at runtime/src/agenc/upstream/components/FileEditToolDiff.tsx as the source of truth, but FileEditToolDiff requires a live edits[] array and reads the file from disk via loadDiffData. AgenC's tool result payload already carries the computed diff string, so the bridge dispatches to a local EditDiffView (in runtime/src/tui/openclaude/tool-stubs.tsx) that mirrors the StructuredDiffList visual contract — bold file header, '+' lines green, '-' lines red, '@@' hunk header cyan, '---'/'+++' file metadata dimmed. Wire shape (<edit-file>/<edit-diff> envelope) stays upstream-compatible so a future flip to the real FileEditToolDiff is a one-file change once options.input is propagated through the bridge.
requiredBehaviors: createBridgeTool('Edit') returns a tool whose renderToolResultMessage dispatches to a local EditDiffView that renders the file path as a bold header and colors '+' lines green, '-' lines red, '@@' hunk headers cyan, '---'/'+++' lines dimmed (visual contract from upstream StructuredDiffList)
The toolResult content shape passed to EditDiffView carries the file path and the diff string in <edit-file>/<edit-diff> tags so the renderer can extract them via extractBridgeTag
If the tool result for Edit lacks the <edit-diff> envelope (e.g. dry-run preview only, or error case), the bridge falls back to the generic <Text> renderer instead of throwing
message-adapter.ts formatStructuredToolResult for tool_call_completed with toolName='Edit' produces the <edit-file>/<edit-diff> envelope shape that EditDiffView consumes
edgeCases: Edit result for a new file (no prior content, diff is all '+' lines) renders all additions in green without crashing on a missing '---' header
Edit result for a file deletion (all '-' lines) renders coherently with all deletions in red
Edit result with a multi-hunk diff renders all hunks, not just the first
Edit result whose diff string is empty falls back to a (No changes) indicator instead of an empty box
Edit result whose path is not a string (number, null, object) — formatStructuredToolResult drops the <edit-file> tag instead of emitting a malformed envelope; the diff body still goes through tagged
Edit result wrapped in the legacy single-string content (from before the structured projection) falls through to the generic Text renderer without crashing

rows.deviation: Same architecture as bash and edit rows: local FileReadView renderer in tool-stubs.tsx instead of importing upstream FileReadTool/UI.tsx (bun:bundle/teamMemPaths chain blocks vitest source resolution). Wire shape (<read-file>, <read-lines>, <read-content> envelope) is upstream-compatible. Live tool name is FileRead (NOT 'Read' as the previous bridge pre-seed claimed); the Read pre-seed has been removed as part of this row.
requiredBehaviors: createBridgeTool('FileRead') routes to FileReadView when content carries the <read-content> envelope; createBridgeTool('Read') (the legacy wrong pre-seed name) does NOT route — exact-case + correct-name dispatch
FileReadView renders the file path bolded, optionally with a [startLine-endLine] range, followed by the file content body
If the content is empty, FileReadView renders an (empty file) indicator instead of an empty Box
message-adapter.formatStructuredToolResult for tool_call_completed with toolName='FileRead' produces the <read-file>/<read-lines>/<read-content> envelope shape FileReadView consumes
Pre-seeded createBridgeTools list now contains 'FileRead' (canonical live name), removing the previously wrong 'Read' pre-seed
edgeCases: FileRead result with content longer than 8KB is truncated to ~8KB with a [N chars truncated] marker (no megabyte-scale renders)
FileRead result missing the path field (slice-only result) renders the body without a header instead of throwing
FileRead result missing the content field (zero-length read) renders the (empty file) indicator
FileRead with the legacy single-string content shape (no envelope) falls through to the generic Text renderer without crashing
FileRead with non-string startLine/endLine falls back to omitting the line range header instead of emitting <read-lines> with non-numeric values
createBridgeTool('Read') (the wrong pre-seed name) does NOT route to FileReadView even with the correct envelope content

rows.deviation: Local FileWriteView renderer in tool-stubs.tsx mirrors upstream FileWriteTool/UI.tsx visual contract (path header + green summary line). Wire shape (<write-file>/<write-summary>) is upstream-compatible.
requiredBehaviors: createBridgeTool('Write') routes to FileWriteView when content carries the <write-summary> envelope
FileWriteView renders the file path bolded plus a green summary line with the byte count when present
If only the path or only the byte count is available, FileWriteView still produces a coherent summary (e.g. 'Wrote file' fallback)

message-adapter.formatStructuredToolResult for tool_call_completed with toolName='Write' computes a byte count from result.bytesWritten OR result.content.length and produces the <write-file>/<write-summary> envelope
edgeCases: Write result with bytesWritten present takes precedence over content.length for the byte count
Write result with content but no bytesWritten falls back to content.length
Write result missing both bytesWritten and content produces a 'Wrote file' summary without a byte count
Write result missing the path field still renders the green summary line (no header)
Write with the legacy single-string content shape falls through to generic Text without crashing
Write result with bytesWritten=1 produces 'Wrote 1 byte' (singular), not 'Wrote 1 bytes'

rows.deviation: Local GrepMatchesView renderer in tool-stubs.tsx mirrors upstream GrepTool/UI.tsx visual contract (bold pattern header with match count + one Text child per match). Wire shape (<grep-pattern>/<grep-matches>) is upstream-compatible. Match line format is `file:line:content`.
requiredBehaviors: createBridgeTool('Grep') routes to GrepMatchesView when content carries the <grep-matches> envelope
GrepMatchesView renders the bold pattern header with a (N matches) count and one Text child per match line
If matches are empty, GrepMatchesView renders a (no matches) indicator with the pattern header preserved
Large match lists are truncated to 200 visible matches plus a dim '… N more matches truncated' marker

message-adapter.formatStructuredToolResult for tool_call_completed with toolName='Grep' walks result.matches OR result.results, projects each {file, line, content} entry as 'file:line:content', and produces the <grep-pattern>/<grep-matches> envelope
edgeCases: Grep result with matches as an array of strings (already pre-formatted) preserves them verbatim
Grep result with matches as objects produces 'file:line:content' lines
Grep result with the alternate `text` field (instead of `content`) on each match still resolves the line content
Grep result with empty matches array produces (no matches) indicator
Grep result with > 200 matches truncates with N-more-truncated marker
Grep result missing the pattern field omits the header but still renders match lines

rows.deviation: Local GlobPathsView renderer in tool-stubs.tsx mirrors upstream GlobTool/UI.tsx visual contract (bold pattern header + path list). Wire shape (<glob-pattern>/<glob-paths>) is upstream-compatible.
requiredBehaviors: createBridgeTool('Glob') routes to GlobPathsView when content carries the <glob-paths> envelope
GlobPathsView renders the bold pattern header with a (N paths) count and one Text child per path
If paths are empty, GlobPathsView renders a (no paths) indicator with the pattern header preserved
Large path lists are truncated to 200 visible paths plus a dim '… N more paths truncated' marker

message-adapter.formatStructuredToolResult for tool_call_completed with toolName='Glob' accepts result.paths OR result.files OR a bare array result, and produces the <glob-pattern>/<glob-paths> envelope
edgeCases: Glob result with paths as an array directly (no { paths } wrapper) still produces the envelope
Glob result with paths as { files: [...] } (alternate field name) still resolves
Glob result with empty paths array produces (no paths) indicator
Glob result with > 200 paths truncates with N-more-truncated marker
Glob result missing the pattern field omits the header but still renders path lines
Glob result with a single path produces 'path' (singular), not 'paths', in the header

rows.deviation: Cross-cutting error renderer (ToolErrorView) in tool-stubs.tsx mirrors upstream FallbackToolUseErrorMessage visual contract (red-bold header + dimmed message body). Wire shape (<tool-error-name>/<tool-error>) is upstream-compatible. The bridge tool also exposes renderToolUseErrorMessage so upstream's error channel reaches the same renderer.
requiredBehaviors: Any tool with <tool-error> envelope routes to ToolErrorView regardless of tool name (cross-cutting dispatch)
Tool error envelope takes precedence over per-tool envelopes (defensive ordering — a Bash error containing both <bash-stdout> and <tool-error> renders the error view)
createBridgeTool exposes renderToolUseErrorMessage(error) which formats the error message + tool name and dispatches to ToolErrorView, so upstream's error channel reaches the same renderer
ToolErrorView renders a red-bold '<toolName> error' header and the error message body; falls back to generic 'Tool error' header when no <tool-error-name> tag is present
message-adapter.formatStructuredToolError(toolName, message) produces the <tool-error-name>/<tool-error> envelope; omits the name tag when toolName is empty
edgeCases: renderToolUseErrorMessage with a string error produces the envelope with the string as the message
renderToolUseErrorMessage with an Error instance extracts .message
renderToolUseErrorMessage with an arbitrary object falls back to short JSON
Tool error envelope on an unrecognized tool name still routes to ToolErrorView
Tool error envelope wins over per-tool envelope when both present
ToolErrorView with no <tool-error-name> tag renders 'Tool error' header without the tool name prefix

requiredBehaviors: createBridgeTool(<any unrouted name>) returns a tool whose renderToolResultMessage produces the generic <Box><Text>{joined}</Text></Box> output (no routing to BashOutputView or EditDiffView)
Generic fallback handles string content, object content, and array content via the resultTextForBridgeTool helper
Generic fallback never throws on null or undefined content
The dispatch table is name-keyed exact-case; unrouted names do NOT accidentally match a registered renderer (no prefix or substring match across tool names)
Bash and Edit envelope tags do not cross-route — Bash tool name with <edit-diff> content stays generic, Edit tool name with <bash-stdout> stays generic
edgeCases: createBridgeTool('XYZUnknown') receives a structured-content array (from formatStructuredToolResult) and renders by joining the .text fields — does not throw on the unexpected shape
createBridgeTool('') (empty string name) does not throw and renders the generic fallback
createBridgeTool('bash') (lowercase) does NOT route to BashOutputView — name dispatch is exact-case
createBridgeTool('edit') (lowercase) does NOT route to EditDiffView — name dispatch is exact-case
renderToolResultMessage receives null content and renders without throwing
Unrouted tool name with content that contains a <bash-stdout> envelope does NOT route to BashOutputView (envelope-only matching does not bypass the tool-name gate)
Bash tool name with <edit-diff> envelope (cross-routing attempt) stays generic — envelope tag must match tool name
